description: CreateConnector — Databricks oauth-sso (U2M shared refresh token).
notes: UI auth-mode label: 'OAuth (SSO)'. Shared-credential OAuth U2M (user-to-machine) flow — one refresh token serves the whole workspace. Sibling tab 'OAuth (Individual)' is the per-member variant.
authStrategy wire tag: `oauth_sso` (same value Snowflake OAuth SSO uses). Distinct from `service_role` used by Access Token + Client Credentials.

UI/wire label mismatch: UI says 'OAuth (SSO)' but the oneof variant is `oauthU2m` (camelCase wire) / `oauth_u2m` (snake_case server-side) — NOT `oauthSso`. First attempts with `oauthSso` return 400 'databricks.databricks_auth requires pat, client_credentials, or oauth_u2m'. The three valid `databricksAuth` siblings are `pat`, `clientCredentials`, `oauthU2m`. 'U2M' is Databricks' own term for user-to-machine OAuth, as opposed to M2M (mapped by TextQL to `clientCredentials`).

`config.databricks` fields for OAuth SSO: host, httpPath, port, catalog, schema + `databricksAuth.oauthU2m.{clientId, clientSecret}`. UI-required: name, host, httpPath, port, catalog, schema, clientId, clientSecret — 8 fields, same count as Client Credentials. TextQL requires a client secret even though Databricks U2M apps can be public (PKCE); presumably for the server-side refresh-token exchange.

Pending-OAuth state: server accepts CreateConnector without any completed OAuth handshake. The UI disables its Create button in-browser with 'Please complete OAuth authentication', but a direct POST with only clientId/clientSecret + workspace fields returns 200 and persists the connector. Matches Snowflake OAuth SSO behavior — the ana CLI can create SSO connectors headlessly (connector not usable for queries until a human completes the handshake).

No TestConnector: the OAuth (SSO) tab has no 'Test Connection' button — the UI skips pre-create testing entirely (same as Snowflake OAuth modes). The server can't validate OAuth creds without a live refresh token.

Separate RPC service observed: `textql.rpc.public.databricks_oauth.DatabricksOAuthService/GetDatabricksOAuthURL`. Fires when the user clicks 'Authenticate with Databricks'. Request: `{workspaceUrl, clientId, state: <uuid>}`. Equivalent to Snowflake's `SnowflakeOAuthService.GetSnowflakeOAuthURL`. Authorize URL format: `https://<host>/oidc/v1/authorize?client_id=<id>&code_challenge=<S256-pkce>&code_challenge_method=S256&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.textql.com%2Fauth%2Fdatabricks%2Fcallback&response_type=code&scope=all-apis+offline_access&state=<uuid>`. Scopes are fixed as `all-apis offline_access` (no role-based scope like Snowflake). PKCE challenge is generated in-browser. Redirect URI is hard-coded to `https://app.textql.com/auth/databricks/callback`. Worth cataloging separately if needed.

GetConnector persisted shape: `connector.databricksMetadata.{host, httpPath, port, databricksAuth, catalog, schema}`. `databricksAuth.oauthU2m.clientId` IS echoed; `clientSecret` is absent. `connector.authStrategy` = `oauth_sso` at top level.

Field diff vs Client Credentials mode: identical shape except `databricksAuth.clientCredentials.{clientId, clientSecret}` becomes `databricksAuth.oauthU2m.{clientId, clientSecret}` and `authStrategy` flips from `service_role` to `oauth_sso`. No new top-level fields introduced.

Databricks-side prerequisite: a confidential OAuth app registered in Databricks (Workspace → Settings → Identity & access → OAuth apps) with SQL-warehouse access. The `clientId`/`clientSecret` come from that app. Distinct from Service Principal OAuth secrets used by Client Credentials mode.
